title: 驱动现代人工智能系统的架构解析：QCon San Francisco 2025
author: Artenisa Chatziou
content:
Senior software engineers are being asked to design and verify AI architectures without established patterns, modernize systems under delivery pressure, and make long-term technical decisions while core technologies are still evolving. With AI adoption accelerating faster than best practices can emerge, teams need validated patterns from production systems, not experimental frameworks. The gap isn't conceptual; it's implementation: How do you actually build reliable AI infrastructure at scale? How do you de-risk agentic systems? What does a production RAG pipeline look like at 100 million users?

QCon San Francisco 2025 (November 17–21) brings together practitioners who've already solved these problems in production. The sessions focus on architectural decisions, implementation constraints, and lessons learned from teams at Netflix, Meta, Intuit, Anthropic, and others. 

Early bird pricing ends November 11.

AI Sessions: Implementation Over Theory

"Senior engineers don't need another talk on 'what is GenAI'", said Hien Luu, QCon Program Committee member. "They're asking: 'How do I build production-grade infrastructure for this? How do I validate my architecture for an agentic system?' These sessions answer those questions from people who've already built it".

What's covered:

  • GenAI infrastructure at 100M-user scale: Maggie Hu, Group Product Manager for the Core AI Platform, and Merrin Kurian, Distinguished Engineer for AI Foundations at Intuit, break down their production stack: vector stores, prompt management, RAG pipelines.
  • Agentic system architecture: Adam Wolff, Engineer on Claude Code at Anthropic, shares the architectural tradeoffs from building the first agentically accelerated software project. Speed over complexity is the theme.
  • Scaling AI org capabilities: Amit Navindgi, Staff Software Engineer leading Zoox Intelligence at Zoox, has a practical blueprint with design patterns for scaling AI capabilities and developer productivity.
  • Post-training techniques for production LLMs: Faye Zhang (Staff Engineer, GenAI search lead) from Pinterest and Andi Partovi (Co-Founder, Veris AI; former GenAI Solution Architect, Google) walk through the techniques that make LLMs actually work in production.
  • AI platforms for reliability: Aaron Erickson, Senior Manager and Founder of DGX Cloud Applied AI Lab at NVIDIA, explores how to design platforms that balance deterministic tools with exploratory agents.
  • Reinforcement Learning for generative ad text: Alex Nikulkov, Research Scientist (RL lead for Monetization GenAI) at Meta, shares how they improved ad generation using reinforcement learning.
  • Choosing AI copilots for productivity: Sepehr Khosravi, ML Platform Engineer at Coinbase, provides a framework for evaluating and selecting AI copilots. Useful if you're trying to figure out which tool to standardize on.
  • Explore the full AI schedule.

Beyond AI: Architecture, Platform Engineering, Reliability

Platform Engineering & CI/CD

  • Keeping CI green across multiple language monorepos: Dhruva Juloori, Senior Software Engineer and core SubmitQueue contributor at Uber, walks through MergeQueue, the CI scheduling system that keeps Uber's mainline green while processing hundreds of changes per hour across Java, Kotlin, Swift, Go, Python, and more. If you're dealing with polyglot monorepos, this one's directly applicable.
  • Automating changes across a diverse fleet: Casey Bleifer, Senior Software Engineer at Netflix, covers how to maintain up-to-date and secure software at scale across polyglot environments without disrupting productivity. Automated fleet management without the chaos.

Architecture & Scalability

  • Building resilient, mission-critical platforms: Matthew Liste, Head of Infrastructure at American Express, has been building infrastructure at American Express for over 20 years, including electronic trading and credit card processing. His talk distills lessons on sustaining platforms long-term, balancing currency with client disruptions, and maintaining security, stability, and scalability. This kind of long-view perspective is rare.
  • Designing a centralized data deletion platform at Netflix scale: Vidhya Arvind, Tech Lead & Founding Architect for Netflix's Data Abstraction Platform, and Shawn Liu, Senior Software Engineer at Netflix, share how they architected a centralized data deletion platform. The talk covers orchestration, observability, journaling, and the tradeoffs between throughput, safety, and scalability under live traffic.
  • Modernizing relevance systems for 1B users: Nishant Lakshmikanth, Engineering Manager at LinkedIn, walks through their multi-year migration from offline batch systems to real-time recommendations serving nearly a billion users. Four migration phases, decoupling candidate generation from scoring, Embedding-Based Retrieval for cold-start, and LLM-powered ranking that cut offline compute costs by 90%+ while boosting engagement.

Security

  • Securing AI-accelerated development: Sriram Madapusi Vasudevan from AWS Agentic AI covers threat-modeling AI pipelines, guarding sensitive data, detecting prompt-injection, and validating AI-generated code before it merges. Particularly relevant given how fast teams are shipping AI features.
  • View the full conference schedule.

QCon San Francisco 2025 runs November 17–21. Early bird pricing ends November 11. View the full schedule and register at qconsf.com.
aiSummary: QCon San Francisco 2025 汇聚行业实践者，探讨人工智能、平台工程、架构及安全等领域在生产规模上的实际挑战。
oneSentenceSummary: QCon San Francisco 2025 汇聚行业实践者，探讨人工智能、平台工程、架构及安全等领域在生产规模上的实际挑战。
summary: 本文宣布 QCon San Francisco 2025 技术大会将于 2025 年 11 月 17 日至 21 日举行。大会面向高级软件工程师，侧重实际应用而非理论探讨。本次大会旨在提供来自生产系统的经过验证的模式和解决方案，专门解决大规模构建可靠的人工智能基础设施、降低 Agentic Systems 风险以及部署生产 RAG 管道的挑战。以人工智能为重点的关键会议包括 Intuit 的 1 亿用户规模的 GenAI 基础设施、Anthropic 的 Agentic System 架构、Zoox 的扩展人工智能组织能力，以及 Pinterest 和 Veris AI 的生产 LLM 的后训练技术。除了人工智能之外，本次大会还涵盖平台工程与 CI/CD（例如，Uber、Netflix）、架构与可扩展性（例如，American Express、Netflix、LinkedIn）和安全（例如，AWS）等关键主题。文章强调，这些会议的演讲者来自 Netflix、Meta、Intuit 和 Anthropic 等领先公司，分享了真实的架构决策和经验教训。文章还包括有关早鸟价格的信息。

mainPoints.point: QCon SF 2025 专注于针对复杂人工智能和软件工程挑战的实用、生产级解决方案。
mainPoints.explanation: 本次大会旨在满足行业对可扩展人工智能基础设施的需求，提供经过验证的模式和实施策略，侧重实际应用而非理论概念。

mainPoints.point: 本次活动以关键的人工智能主题为特色，包括 Intuit 的大规模 GenAI 基础设施和 Anthropic 的 Agentic System 架构。
mainPoints.explanation: 来自领先科技公司的专家将分享他们在构建和验证生产级人工智能系统方面的经验，涵盖 RAG 管道和 LLM 后训练技术等领域。

mainPoints.point: 除了人工智能之外，本次大会还涵盖平台、架构和安全等重要的软件工程领域。
mainPoints.explanation: 与会者可以找到有关在多语言 Monorepos 中维护 CI/CD、设计有弹性的关键任务平台、构建可扩展的数据删除系统以及保护人工智能加速开发的见解。

keyQuotes: 高级工程师不需要另一个关于“什么是 GenAI”的演讲……他们会问：“我如何为此构建生产级基础设施？我如何验证我的 Agentic System 架构？”
差距不在于概念，而在于实施：*你如何真正构建大规模的可靠人工智能基础设施？你如何降低 Agentic Systems 的风险？在 1 亿用户的情况下，生产 RAG 管道是什么样的？*
QCon San Francisco 2025... 汇集了已经在生产中解决了这些问题的实践者。
